title: Departmental Performance Against Cabinet Secretary Objectives 2026-2027
source: Illustrative data for Kew Away Day exercise
note: This is dummy data for demonstration purposes only. It does not represent real performance assessments.

rating_scale.Green: On track — strong delivery against this objective
rating_scale.Amber-Green: Mostly on track — minor issues being managed

1_delivery.summary: Strong coordination of PM priorities across Whitehall. New delivery tracking system operational.

2_policy_advice.summary: High-quality briefings and policy options consistently delivered. Propriety advice timely.

3_cabinet_government.summary: Cabinet Committee programme running well. Cabinet Manual refresh in progress but behind schedule.

4_cs_reform.summary: Leading cross-government reform programme. AI adoption ahead of target. SCS talent framework being piloted.

5_culture.summary: People Survey engagement score improved 3 points. Civil Service Code refresh consultation launched.

1_delivery.summary: Spending Review 2025 delivered on time. Fiscal rules framework embedded. Growth agenda well supported.

2_policy_advice.summary: Budget 2025 delivered with strong analytical base. International engagement effective.

3_cabinet_government.summary: Effective Treasury input to Cabinet committees. Collective agreement processes well managed.

4_cs_reform.summary: Efficiency delivery plans published. AI adoption progressing but below stretch target.

5_culture.summary: Engagement improving but retention of mid-career analysts remains a challenge.

1_delivery.summary: Asylum backlog reducing but still significant. Border security programme on track. Crime reduction targets mixed.

2_policy_advice.summary: Immigration policy advice responsive. Cross-cutting security advice well coordinated with NSA.

3_cabinet_government.summary: Active contributor to NSC and domestic committees. Some delays in collective write-rounds.

4_cs_reform.summary: Large legacy IT estate slowing AI adoption. Workforce plan drafted but implementation delayed. Admin savings behind target.

5_culture.summary: People Survey scores below CS average. Staff wellbeing programme expanded. Leadership visibility improving.

departments.name: Department of Health and Social Care

1_delivery.summary: NHS waiting list reduction on trajectory but fragile. Social care reform progressing. Neighbourhood health framework published.

2_policy_advice.summary: Strong analytical capability. Ten-year health plan evidence base well developed.

3_cabinet_government.summary: Good engagement with social policy committees. Some tension on spending allocation timelines.

4_cs_reform.summary: AI in clinical pathways showing promise. Admin spend reduction on track. Workforce plan needs acceleration.

5_culture.summary: Engagement score stable. Innovation culture improving through health tech partnerships.

1_delivery.summary: Curriculum reform on track. Childcare expansion progressing. School attendance improving but below target.

2_policy_advice.summary: Strong evidence-based policy development. Skills agenda well articulated across government.

3_cabinet_government.summary: Effective cross-Whitehall working on skills and opportunity agendas.

4_cs_reform.summary: Good progress on AI in grants management. Apprenticeship strategy being refreshed internally.

5_culture.summary: High engagement department. Strong internal innovation culture. Diversity targets on track.

departments.name: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

1_delivery.summary: AI Safety Institute fully operational. R&D investment pipeline strong. Tech sector engagement excellent.

2_policy_advice.summary: Leading cross-government AI policy. International tech diplomacy highly effective.

3_cabinet_government.summary: Strong input to economic and science committees. Good interdepartmental coordination on digital.

4_cs_reform.summary: Highest AI adoption rate in Whitehall. Leading by example on digital transformation. Efficiency targets exceeded.

5_culture.summary: Consistently high engagement. Strong innovation culture. Attracting top STEM talent.

1_delivery.summary: Trade deal pipeline progressing. Industrial strategy published. Investment zones showing early results.

2_policy_advice.summary: Pro-growth advice well received. Business engagement framework effective.

3_cabinet_government.summary: Good engagement on economic committees. Trade negotiations well coordinated with FCDO.

4_cs_reform.summary: AI adoption in trade analysis growing. Merger of legacy BIS/DIT systems ongoing.

5_culture.summary: Post-merger culture embedding well. Overseas network morale improving.

departments.name: Department for Environment, Food and Rural Affairs

1_delivery.summary: Environmental Land Management scheme scaling. Water quality targets behind. Flood resilience investment on track.

2_policy_advice.summary: Strong science base. Food security advice well integrated with wider agenda.

3_cabinet_government.summary: Active on climate and rural committees. Some delays on cross-departmental water strategy.

4_cs_reform.summary: AI adoption below target due to specialist workforce needs. Digital transformation of farming payments progressing.

5_culture.summary: Good engagement among policy staff. Field force morale stable. Innovation pilots in satellite monitoring.

1_delivery.summary: Defence spending increase committed. Equipment plan pressures remain. AUKUS programme on track.

2_policy_advice.summary: SDR implementation advice strong. Integrated review well coordinated with NSA.

3_cabinet_government.summary: Excellent NSC engagement. Crisis response capability well maintained.

4_cs_reform.summary: Legacy systems constraining digital transformation. AI adoption in logistics showing potential but slow rollout.

5_culture.summary: Civilian-military culture integration ongoing. Engagement scores below CS average but improving.

1_delivery.summary: International partnerships agenda strong. Development programme realignment underway.

2_policy_advice.summary: Excellent geopolitical analysis. PM well briefed on international issues.

3_cabinet_government.summary: NSC and international committees well served. Summit preparation consistently strong.

4_cs_reform.summary: Global IT estate modernisation complex. AI translation tools deployed. Admin savings on track.

5_culture.summary: Merger culture bedding in well. Overseas posting morale improved after pay review.

1_delivery.summary: Universal Credit migration on track. Employment support programmes expanding. Disability benefits reform underway.

2_policy_advice.summary: Labour market analysis strong. Health and disability policy advice improving.

3_cabinet_government.summary: Social policy committees well served. Good cross-government work on employment.

4_cs_reform.summary: AI fraud detection saving significant sums. Large operational workforce makes transformation complex but progressing.

5_culture.summary: Largest department faces engagement challenges. Frontline staff satisfaction programme launched.

1_delivery.summary: Tax gap narrowing. Digital tax accounts expanding. Customer service improving after difficult period.

2_policy_advice.summary: Tax policy advice high quality. Strong analytical base for fiscal events.

3_cabinet_government.summary: Effective partnership with HMT on fiscal policy. Good committee engagement.

4_cs_reform.summary: Strong digital transformation track record. AI in compliance checking proving effective.

5_culture.summary: Large operational workforce. Regional office consolidation causing uncertainty. New Ways of Working programme launched.

1_delivery.summary: Prison capacity programme expanding. Court backlog reducing but slowly. Probation reforms embedding.

2_policy_advice.summary: Sentencing review well managed. Justice system reform advice drawing on international comparisons.

3_cabinet_government.summary: Good engagement on home affairs and justice committees. Constitution and rule of law advice valued.

4_cs_reform.summary: HMCTS digital modernisation continuing. AI adoption limited by security requirements in prisons estate.

5_culture.summary: Frontline prison and probation staff engagement challenging. HQ innovation culture strengthening.

1_delivery.summary: Major road and rail investment on track. Bus franchising expanding. Decarbonisation targets being pursued.

2_policy_advice.summary: Growth-focused transport investment advice well received. Good regional connectivity analysis.

3_cabinet_government.summary: Effective on economic and infrastructure committees. HS2 lessons learned process underway.

4_cs_reform.summary: AI adoption in network planning growing. Arm's length body reform complex but progressing.

5_culture.summary: Good HQ engagement. ALB culture integration an ongoing focus.

departments.name: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

1_delivery.summary: GB Energy operational. Clean power 2030 target ambitious but plan published. Warm Homes Plan launched.

2_policy_advice.summary: Strong analytical base for energy transition. International climate engagement effective.

3_cabinet_government.summary: Good engagement on climate and energy committees. Cross-government net zero coordination improving.

4_cs_reform.summary: New department still building systems. AI adoption in grid modelling advanced.

5_culture.summary: Mission-driven culture strong. High engagement as a newer department with clear purpose.

departments.name: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

1_delivery.summary: Housing targets ambitious. Planning reform progressing. Building safety regime implemented. Local growth partnerships expanding.

2_policy_advice.summary: Devolution and local growth advice well developed. Housing market analysis strong.

3_cabinet_government.summary: Effective on domestic committees. Central-local relations advice valued.

4_cs_reform.summary: Digital planning tools in development. AI adoption moderate. Admin savings on track.

5_culture.summary: Stable engagement. Good regional office presence. Innovation in data-driven local government support.

departments.name: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

1_delivery.summary: Creative industries growth plan delivering. Sport and physical activity strategy on track.

2_policy_advice.summary: Strong creative economy analysis. Cultural diplomacy advice well received.

3_cabinet_government.summary: Good committee engagement on digital and cultural policy.

4_cs_reform.summary: Small agile department. Digital-first approach embedded. AI used in grants assessment.

5_culture.summary: Small department with strong team culture. High engagement and low attrition.

1_delivery.summary: Stormont institutions supported effectively. Legacy Act implementation progressing.

2_policy_advice.summary: NI political landscape advice timely and well-judged. Constitutional advice strong.

3_cabinet_government.summary: NI perspective well represented in Cabinet discussions.

4_cs_reform.summary: Small department — reform agenda proportionate. Shared services model working well.

5_culture.summary: Small, cohesive team with strong purpose. Excellent engagement scores.

1_delivery.summary: Legal advice turnaround times improved. Litigation outcomes strong.

2_policy_advice.summary: High-quality legal advice across government. Vires and propriety advice consistently strong.

3_cabinet_government.summary: Legal quality assurance of Cabinet papers effective. Constitutional advice valued.

4_cs_reform.summary: AI-assisted legal research tools deployed. Efficiency gains through technology.

5_culture.summary: Professional culture with strong identity. Good retention of legal talent.

1_delivery.summary: Threat disruption rate at highest level. Intelligence product quality consistently strong. AUKUS Pillar 2 technology sharing on track.

2_policy_advice.summary: Timely and relevant intelligence informing PM and NSC decision-making. Joint Intelligence Committee outputs highly valued.

3_cabinet_government.summary: Excellent NSC engagement. Crisis response and COBRA support consistently strong. Intelligence sharing with allies well managed.

4_cs_reform.summary: AI adoption advanced in analytical tradecraft. Workforce diversification progressing. Security constraints limit some cross-government technology sharing.

5_culture.summary: Strong mission-driven culture and loyalty. Retention good but recruitment pipeline needs broadening. Inclusion and openness improving steadily.

1_delivery.summary: Programme for Government delivery progressing. NHS Scotland recovery plan showing improvement. Economic challenges remain with fiscal constraints.

2_policy_advice.summary: Strong policy development capability. Distinctive Scottish approaches well evidenced. Good use of international comparators through Nordic and small-nation networks.

3_cabinet_government.summary: Scottish Cabinet processes functioning well internally. Intergovernmental relations through Interministerial Standing Committee improved but pace of engagement from Whitehall remains a frustration.

4_cs_reform.summary: Digital Scotland strategy progressing. AI adoption at 29% — below stretch target. Workforce planning underway but efficiencies harder to achieve with tight fiscal settlement.

5_culture.summary: People Survey engagement stable. Strong public service ethos. Senior leadership refresh underway following period of change. Values and ethics framework well embedded.

1_delivery.summary: Programme for Government delivery broadly on track. NHS Wales waiting times improving but fragile. Economic Mission showing early results. Intergovernmental coordination strengthened through new frameworks.

2_policy_advice.summary: Strong policy advice to First Minister and Cabinet. Distinctive Welsh policy development well evidenced. Good use of Well-being of Future Generations framework for long-term thinking.

3_cabinet_government.summary: Welsh Cabinet and committee processes functioning well. Intergovernmental relations through Interministerial Standing Committee improving but some frustrations remain on pace of Whitehall engagement.

4_cs_reform.summary: AI adoption at 32% — above some larger departments but below stretch target. Digital strategy for Wales progressing. Efficiency savings on track but workforce planning needs acceleration. Legacy IT systems being modernised.

5_culture.summary: People Survey engagement at 64, up 2 points. Strong Welsh identity and bilingual culture. Social partnership model well embedded. Senior leadership pipeline being strengthened.
